records.summary: Router work needs route-config, route-tree, route-context, viewport, viewport-agent, routeable-component, and route-recognizer semantics before product features rely on it.
records.rationale: External apps are pressure floors, not literal specs. Router modeling must be grounded in the Aurelia framework router architecture because semantic-runtime will eventually support MCP, IDE, AOT, SSR, and SSG.
guidance: Start with framework.router flow and relationship projections before deriving product router behavior from an app sample.

Router URL parsing and stringifying are first-class `framework.router` flow rows under the `url-parsing` stage with `url-parser` relationship mechanisms; do not treat route-recognizer rows as the whole navigation URL story.

Treat `framework.router:flow-issues` and `framework.router:recognizer-issues` as map freshness checks too: source-baseline and descriptor-count drift are issue rows, not only materialization warnings.
Use `pnpm --filter @aurelia-ls/atlas framework:router -- --projection=relationships --query=ViewportAgent --detail` when row-level router grounding is needed beyond the compact pressure summary.

Use `pnpm --filter @aurelia-ls/atlas bridge:aulink -- --projection=mirror --packageId=router --detail` when the question is whether router framework actors have product mirrors, role evidence, or emulation obligations.
Model au-viewport explicitly when route rendering semantics are in scope.
Treat route-recognizer as a potentially wide substrate task, similar in complexity to expression or attribute parsing.

Runtime-state-specific recursive expansion is a prerequisite for router/viewport work only when the product asks for per-activation truth such as exact active viewport trees, activation/reuse/deactivate ordering, SSR/SSG route render output, or route-driven stateful recursive component expansion. Static route config, recognizer, route tree, parameter, typed navigation, and authoring guidance should remain finite and definition-level when possible.

Router raw Error rows in ComponentAgent, ViewportInstructionTree.toUrl, and ViewportAgent._canUnload are intentionally unclaimed live state-machine guards as of 2026-05-14; do not turn them into diagnostics unless semantic-runtime grows the corresponding imperative navigation/controller-state emulator.

records.summary: RouteConfigKernelEmitter is the route-config convergence frontier for route config products, routeable components, validation issues, fallback components, child routes, field provenance, and kernel records.
records.rationale: External app pressure often starts from authored route arrays or decorators, but product semantics need the route-config model before route tree, route context, viewport, or route-recognizer layers can consume it. Keep this emitter explicit so RouteableComponent can become a convergeable thing rather than an app-shape heuristic, and so router validation failures are source-backed before normalized route facts flow downstream.
guidance: Start here when authored route configs, child routes, component/fallback values, RouteableComponent products, or route-config and child-route configuration diagnostics need better provenance or type handling.

Spend router validation codes only for statically closed values matching validation.ts: rtInvalidConfigProperty / AUR3554 for invalid property values and rtInvalidConfig / AUR3555 for null config objects. Weak/external route config values should stay open instead of becoming guessed diagnostics.

Route object vocabulary is validation authority too: unknown ordinary route-config keys spend rtUnknownConfigProperty / AUR3556, while redirect route objects only allow path and redirectTo and spend rtUnknownRedirectConfigProperty / AUR3557 for everything else.

Spend RouteConfigContext._configureChildRoutes authority for source-backed child route objects whose component is a lazy import and whose path property is absent: rcNoPathLazyImport / AUR3173 belongs here, not in route-tree materialization.

Spend RouteContext._resolveLazy invalid-import authority only for definitely invalid lazy-import fulfillments: rcInvalidLazyImport / AUR3175 is exact when a closed module has no resource definition, no partial custom-element-definition name, and no class/function export that a stronger resource-recognition pass might later identify.

String routeable component resolution consumes ResourceDefinitionIndex custom-element names, aliases, and dependency-scoped custom-element lookup. `router-contextual-string-routeable` keeps the parent component dependencies disambiguation canary alive, while `router-route-config-validation-errors` keeps the no-known-component AUR3552 canary alive.

The remaining string routeable frontier is full RouteConfigContext resource visibility: root-container registrations, imported view resources, inline templates, and no-context string/lazy-import guards such as AUR3551/AUR3553. If an imported routeable component source is present but invisible to the resource index, inspect shared static-evaluation side-effect import execution before adding router-local scanning.

RouteConfig rows now carry separate origin and value-shape dimensions. Use originKind to distinguish @route, Route.configure, class static defaults, and child-route sources; use valueKind to distinguish object/path/routeable/static-default/open shapes. Authoring navigation taste should expose origin-shaped values separately from static/dynamic value-shape readings instead of inferring policy from route existence.

Generated route-config/decorator source should route through router-owned source serializers such as `route-configuration-source.ts`; app-builder can select route-shell compositions, but the source syntax for `@route(...)` and route object literals belongs with router semantics.
Preserve framework property paths such as `routes[0].path` and source ranges on validation issues; these are diagnostics and future repair anchors, not only pressure counts.
Keep route-config convergence separate from route-tree transition and route-recognizer state graph semantics.
Use framework.router route-config, routeable-component, route-context, and viewport-agent projections before changing product router behavior.

anchors.summary: Product-free router source serializer for generated route decorator object-literal configs.

records.summary: RouteRecognizerStateGraphBuilder is the current product pressure point for route-recognizer graph semantics.
records.rationale: Route-recognizer has a high complexity ceiling similar to parsers. Product behavior should be grounded in framework.router recognizer rows before app samples drive assumptions.
guidance: Start from framework.router recognizer and recognizer-issues projections.

Check `framework.errors` with `gap=raw-error-usage` and `projection=semantic-raw-references` when route-recognizer semantics become diagnostic or policy work; route-recognizer currently exposes message-expression raw Error usage rather than framework ErrorNames.

Known framework-throwing recognizer registration conditions should become RouteRecognizerIssue products and API rows, not open seams or silently accepted graphs. Since @aurelia/route-recognizer throws raw Error instances rather than mapped AUR codes, expose modeled issues as framework-runtime-behavior, keep framework-error-code null, and cite exact `frameworkRawErrorAuthority(...)` constants when the framework throw site is part of `framework.errors`.

Reserved `$$residue` parameter usage and invalid route-parameter regex constraints are registration issues; do not materialize endpoint/state graph products for paths the framework would reject during `RouteRecognizer.$add(...)`.

Route pattern, endpoint, and recognized-route API rows now preserve parameter-name groups. Recognized-route and route-node rows preserve decoded parameter values, recursive residual child recognition publishes nested child recognized routes from routed child RouteConfigContexts, and route-node rows expose child-first / parent-first / append / by-route aggregates over the materialized route-node parent chain. Closed static query params flow through ViewportInstructionTree, RouteTree, and RouteNode rows; repeated query keys stay visible as row entries and as multi-value aggregate arrays in include-query strategies. Include-query append/by-route rows intentionally repeat shared query values per active route node because Aurelia writes ViewportInstructionTree query params onto every node before route-context aggregation. Route-config, router-viewport, viewport-instruction-tree, route-node, component-agent, and router-options rows carry enough named viewport/query/fragment/activeClass shape for generated recipes to verify sibling viewport-instruction targets, low-boilerplate active link styling, and static include-query route parameter reads. Start from those rows when an app-pattern fixture needs static parameter or viewport contract verification. The `router-active-link-state` pressure fixture proves `load.active.bind` as a controller-view-model from-view raw-property channel and `active.class` as a normal class-toggle channel; dynamic query merging, broader live active-navigation state, and viewport activation remain route-context/runtime frontiers.
Treat endpoint registration, path grammar, state graph, candidate selection, and cache behavior as separate semantic axes.
Do not collapse recognizer work into generic route-config traversal.

anchors.summary: Project pass that parses configurable routes and emits recognizer state graphs.

records.summary: RouteTreeTransitionMaterializationFrame is a live pressure point for route tree transition semantics, redirect migration diagnostics, and route-node handoff.
records.rationale: Route tree work overlaps controller semantics, viewport agents, routeable components, recursive rendering, and framework-thrown router errors. It should be tracked as a first-class frontier rather than inferred from broad router notes.
guidance: Check framework.router route-tree, viewport-agent, and relationship rows before changing product route-tree materialization.
RouteableComponent should be treated as a convergeable thing like CustomElement when component discovery is in scope.
Keep au-viewport semantics explicit when route rendering or recursive hydration is involved.

RouteTree redirect-parameter migration accepts only Segment / slash-scoped Segment RouteExpression chains. Sibling composites and grouped expressions belong in RouterIssue rows with exact router exprUnexpectedKind / AUR3502 authority, not route-recognizer rows or generic open seams.

Closed static redirect targets must carry redirectSourceRouteConfig from RouteRecognizer expansion into RouteTree compilation. Use that exact edge to suppress generic redirect-target open seams only when the redirect target was actually re-recognized.
Unknown static redirect targets belong to instrUnknownRedirect / AUR3402 RouterIssue rows from redirect expansion, not to generic redirect-target open seams.

Residual parent-route matches should not stop at the parent residual parameter. RouteRecognitionMaterializationProjectPass follows the residual child ViewportInstruction through the resolved child RouteConfigContext so RouteTreeTransitionMaterializationFrame can materialize nested child RouteNode and ComponentAgent products under the parent route context.

A closed ViewportRequest with no matching available ViewportAgent is an exact router issue with rcNoAvailableVpa / AUR3174 authority, not a route-tree open seam. Do not publish a partial transition tree for that chain, and keep genuinely missing request/context handoffs as open seams.

records.summary: RouteConfigContextMaterializationProjectPass materializes RouteConfigContext topology and owned RouteRecognizer instances from route configs.
records.rationale: Route contexts sit between authored route configs, recognizer ownership, eager-loading policy, and later runtime topology. Keep this layer explicit instead of folding route-context construction into recognizer or route-tree passes.
guidance: Start here when route-config roots, child context trees, eager-loading recognizer ownership, or RouteConfigContext products change.
Keep RouteConfigContext materialization separate from authored route-config convergence and runtime RouteContext/ViewportAgent topology.
Use framework.router route-context and route-tree rows before changing context ownership semantics.

anchors.summary: Framework router relationship rows for route-context semantics.

records.summary: RouteRuntimeTopologyProjectPass and RouteRuntimeTopologyFrame materialize RouteContext, au-viewport, ViewportCustomElement, ViewportAgent, and route child-container topology.
records.rationale: The user called out au-viewport and ViewportAgent as first-class router semantics. This topology lane is the bridge from static template rendering/controller products into route runtime context products before navigation or activation.
guidance: Start here when work touches au-viewport, ViewportAgent, RouteContext, route child containers, or static router/rendering topology.
Keep runtime topology separate from route-config contexts, route instruction parsing, recognition, and route-tree transition materialization.
Use framework.router viewport-agent and framework.rendering controller rows before changing route rendering semantics.

anchors.summary: Framework router viewport-agent and route-context relationship rows.

records.summary: RouteInstructionMaterializationProjectPass closes static load/href router resource values into typed navigation and viewport instruction products.
records.rationale: Route instruction materialization depends on rendered router resources, binding source value evaluation, route contexts, and router options. Keep this as an explicit router/template bridge instead of treating load/href as plain attribute strings.
guidance: Start here when load/href custom attributes, object navigation instructions, typed navigation instructions, viewport instruction trees, or open route-expression seams change.

Router resource instructions should materialize only when a concrete owning RouteContext is known through controller/container ancestry or route-config component-definition matching. A reusable component's standalone definition render is not an app-level route instruction site; do not publish a route-context open seam when the same instruction closes under a routed parent render.
Use observation source-value evaluation and template controller/rendering products before changing static router instruction closure.

For dynamic href seams, mirror HrefCustomAttribute exactly: `_resolveIsExternal(...)` gates URL generation, while RouterOptions.useHref=false, non-anchor hosts, or a co-located load attribute only disable click interception and must not be treated as proof of external URL intent.

Anchor `target` values other than `_self` are not automatically disabled in the semantic model: framework `HrefCustomAttribute` disables interception for those targets unless the literal target equals runtime `IWindow.name`. Use `router-href-click-interception-target-open` for that host-contract boundary instead of collapsing `_blank` or named targets into unconditional disabled click handling.

When an open router instruction seam has a binding/value source address, diagnostics-to-action or future edit planning should preserve it as a `runtime-boundary` action target. The target is for intent collection and future planning, not proof that a source edit is already safe.

Use the synthetic `router-dynamic-pattern` pressure fixture before external app pressure when changing href repair behavior; it covers internal string-pattern closure, external-link-like field openness, and bare external-module value boundaries with non-current-window target pressure.

As of the compact app-pressure check on 2026-05-23, the aggregate `router.open-instruction` seam is expected pressure: `router-dynamic-pattern` keeps an external-module href value open while click interception is disabled by `useHref=false` and target comparison remains runtime-open through `router-href-click-interception-target-open`. `router-viewport-resolution-errors` should publish exact AUR3174 `no-available-viewport-agent` diagnostics without adding a parallel repair-intent seam.
Keep route instruction materialization separate from recognizer path matching and route-tree transition compilation.

Closed primitive router-resource values that cannot be route strings, routeable components, or viewport instructions are TypedNavigationInstruction creation issues with instrInvalid / AUR3400 authority. Do not spend this code on classes/functions/promises that are valid framework routeable lanes but currently under-modeled.

Object-form router resource values belong to RouteConfigContext eager path generation before they re-enter the RouteExpression instruction-tree lane. Missing required params for RouteConfig/class components are RouterIssue rows with exact AUR3166 authority; string route ids are framework non-throwing and should not spend that code. Closed instruction children recurse through the generated component RouteConfigContext and preserve child viewport suffixes before lowering back into the static instruction-tree lane.

Inline load multi-bindings with `route` plus object-shaped `params` follow LoadCustomAttribute's component+params branch, not plain RouteExpression parsing. Route-only strings remain RouteExpression input; route+params becomes an eager instruction before static path recognition. Binding-scope param values may use dynamic placeholders when the parameter key is static, but never reuse the route id as the parameter value. Treat `context.bind` as a route-context replacement boundary and keep it open until semantic-runtime models that owning context.

Object-form router resource values may arrive as syntactic object literals or equivalent binding-source values such as state/getter-owned instruction arrays. Keep those reads in RuntimeBindingSourceValueEvaluator and consume the closed object here instead of adding router-local expression shortcuts.

`routerSourceExpressionEvaluationFrame(...)` is the route-instruction bridge from a rendered router-resource site into RuntimeBindingSourceValueEvaluationContext or an explicit open source-value row. It now spends `projectRuntimeBindingSourceValueContextInScope(...)` with the rendered binding table and binding-expression scope projector; if this starts growing, improve source-value projection/context primitives rather than reintroducing router-local expression evaluation or a router-owned source-expression projector.

`route-instruction-source.ts` is the source companion for router resource attributes. Source producers should use it for `load` and `href` so generated simple and inline multi-binding forms stay aligned with LoadCustomAttribute and HrefCustomAttribute instead of becoming app-builder-local string templates.

Equivalent object-form router resource values may also arrive as evaluator-local class instances or tagged-template/function results. Route instruction materialization should consume the closed EvaluationObject/Instance shape and leave New/TaggedTemplate AST semantics with RuntimeBindingSourceValueEvaluator.

Router-resource binding-source reads can consume DI activation facts when the render site has controller/container ancestry. Registered interface state such as resolve(IFoo) belongs to RuntimeBindingSourceActivationContext plus the modeled container, not to route-instruction materialization.

Router-resource binding scope lookup should use the render-context-aware RuntimeInstructionScopeLookup. Custom-attribute controllers are created from a hydration instruction, but the binding expression scope belongs to the controller that rendered the instruction sequence, including synthetic template-controller child views.

String-pattern route expressions may arrive from interpolation, template literals, evaluator-local methods, or direct binary `+` concatenation with binding-scope holes. Consume the shared EvaluationStringPatternValue lane and keep the authored static-prefix rule in the binding-source evaluator, not in router-specific AST shortcuts.

Repeated state-backed href canaries should close through binding-source representative values, evaluator loose nullish equality, and recognizer matching when the static prefix survives. If exact per-item field correlation is needed later, model that as value-flow substrate pressure rather than href-specific lookup logic.

Use `route-expression-parser.ts` when framework semantics branch on RouteExpression node kind. It exposes the expression tree, lowered viewport instructions, and source-backed parser failures for exprUnexpectedSegment / AUR3500 and exprNotDone / AUR3501; do not reparse route strings with ad hoc split logic.

Router issue products remain owned by the router lane even when their source is a template value. API template diagnostics and cursor-info may project `router-framework-error` rows for LSP loci, while AppDiagnostics de-duplicates those projected rows and reads the owning RouterIssues lane.

Route-instruction issue provenance should prefer the parser expression source or HTML attribute value address over the broader custom-attribute carrier. A broad carrier span is a fallback evidence address, not a rename/edit locus, once value provenance is available.

anchors.summary: Router-resource bridge from rendered instruction/source scope through shared binding-source value context projection into a source-value context or explicit open row.
Framework-shaped RouteConfigContext eager path-generation substrate for object navigation instructions.
Product-free source serializer for router load/href custom-attribute forms.

records.summary: RouteRecognitionMaterializationProjectPass walks route-recognizer graphs for static viewport instruction paths and publishes RecognizedRoute products.
records.rationale: Recognition is algorithmic router substrate: candidates, endpoint requirements, redirect expansion, parameter extraction, and path collapse all need to remain grounded in route-recognizer semantics before route-tree compilation consumes them.
guidance: Start here when static route recognition, candidate selection, redirect expansion, endpoint requirements, or recognized-route products change.
Cross-check framework.router recognizer mechanics before changing Candidate or EndpointRequirement behavior.
Redirect expansion must use the shared route redirect migration substrate rather than splitting redirect strings on `/`; RouteTree is the diagnostic owner for unsupported RouteExpression node kinds.

A closed static viewport-instruction path with a materialized recognizer, no matching configured route, and no owning route-config fallback is a recognition issue with instrNoFallback / AUR3401 authority.

For nested route expressions such as a parent route with residual child segments, recognition recurses into the routed child's RouteConfigContext after the parent residual match. This keeps child RecognizedRoute, RouteNode, and ComponentAgent products visible instead of relying on a single parent residual row.

A recognized redirect route whose migrated target path cannot be recognized is a redirect-resolution issue with instrUnknownRedirect / AUR3402 authority; semantic-runtime records it during redirect expansion even though the framework throws while creating the RouteTree node.
Keep recognition separate from route-recognizer graph construction and route-tree transition materialization.

anchors.summary: Endpoint requirement accumulator for recognized route drafts.

records.summary: SemanticAppRouteQueries is the intentional explicit public query-method surface over the shared route query registry.
records.rationale: Router modeling is broad enough that route-family query methods should stay visible without making runtime.ts restate every route query. The public facade keeps named methods for callers, while route-query-registry owns the shared SemanticAppQueryKind, routeProductKind, row-reader, and answer-label metadata used by API dispatch and route expected-effect facts.
guidance: Start here when a public API query touches route configs, route contexts, recognizer rows, viewport instructions/agents, route trees/nodes, or component agents.
Keep row construction in route-projections and answer-envelope mechanics in answer-helpers; this class should stay a public query-method facade.
If router query pressure expands, split along route-config, recognizer, viewport, route-tree, or agent concepts rather than moving methods back into runtime.ts.
Keep route-effect facts sourced from route-query-registry; authoring verification may derive query-only rows from that registry, but should not restate the route product vocabulary by hand.

Route expected-effect helpers can now filter route product rows by fields such as `parameterNames`. If recipes need richer route-tree shape, viewport selection, runtime parameter aggregation, or issue-specific expectations, extend the route expected-effect helper layer before adding ad hoc recipe filters.

anchors.summary: Shared route query descriptor registry for API dispatch and route expected-effect facts.
Shared route-effect fact stream and route product registry.
Authoring expected-effect helpers for route product families.
API split guidance for route-family answerers.
